citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: Marigold's face brightens immediately. 'Oh, this is exactly what I needed. You didn't have to, but I'm so glad you did.'
Marigold holds it up to the light. 'It's lovely. Where did you find this?' She sets it carefully on the shelf behind the counter.
Marigold smiles warmly. 'Brought in from outside. I can always tell. Thank you.'

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Marigold's smile disappears. 'I... appreciate the thought, but I can't have that in the bakery.' She doesn't take it.
Marigold blinks. 'That's... kind of you. I think.' She takes it, but sets it out of sight.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: 'Now that's useful.' Agnes turns it over in her hands approvingly. 'You've got good instincts.'
Agnes inspects it carefully. 'Where did this come from? Not the market — I'd have noticed.' She's pleased.
Agnes nods. 'I know what this is. Good of you to bring it.' She tucks it away.
'Very pretty.' Agnes says it kindly but without conviction. She's the sort of person who means it when they say they have nowhere to put things.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Agnes looks at it without expression. 'I've seen worse,' she says — which, from Agnes, is almost a compliment. She takes it anyway.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: Eleanor examines it with professional interest. 'This is genuinely useful. Thank you — I'll put it to use.'
Eleanor is quiet for a moment. 'It's quite lovely. I don't often receive things like this.' She holds it carefully.
Eleanor smiles — a smaller, more private smile than most people's. 'That's thoughtful of you.'

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Eleanor's expression doesn't change, but something in her posture does. 'I'm afraid that's not... appropriate for the library.' She does not take it.
Eleanor accepts it with a measured expression. 'You're very kind,' she says, in a tone that means: I will not display this.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: Constance takes it with both hands. 'This is quite fine,' she says — and from Constance, that means something.
Constance nods once. 'Sensible.' She places it in her coat pocket.
Constance accepts it with composed grace. 'Thank you. That's considerate.'
'How thoughtful.' Constance says it neutrally. She accepts.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Constance's face closes. 'I beg your pardon.' She does not take it, and does not elaborate.
Constance considers it for a moment. 'I won't be needing that,' she says with perfect politeness, and that is that.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: Rosalind's eyes sharpen. 'That's actually useful.' She turns it over, checking the construction. 'Good. Thank you.'
Rosalind tilts her head. 'Nice craftsmanship.' Coming from her, that's warm.
Rosalind holds it for a moment. 'From outside the valley?' She accepts.
'I'll have it later.' Rosalind takes it, then probably forgets about it.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Rosalind glances at it. 'No thank you.' She says it without cruelty but also without any ambiguity.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: Clem grins. 'That'll come in handy. Where'd you find it?' He pockets it immediately.
Clem examines it with genuine pleasure. 'Good eye. Not many people notice this sort of thing.'
Clem takes it and sniffs it approvingly. 'Trail food. The best kind.' He's sincere.
Clem looks at it, then at you. 'Seen worse,' he says cheerfully. 'Seen much worse. Thanks.'
Clem holds it politely. 'It's... nice. Sure. Thank you.' He's trying.
Fletcher takes it with both hands. 'Remarkable. Where exactly did you find this? Elevation? Time of day?' He's already making notes.
Fletcher nods seriously. 'This could be useful for the monitoring work.' He accepts.
Fletcher blinks. 'Oh. Yes, it's — lovely. Thank you.' He's not unmoved, just more accustomed to functional beauty.
'Interesting condition,' Fletcher says, examining it with scientific detachment. 'Has it always been like this?'
Fletcher looks slightly startled. 'Thank you. I sometimes forget to eat, so this is... actually useful.'
Prewitt smiles gently. 'That's very kind of you. I'll think of you when I look at it.'
Prewitt turns it over slowly. 'There's something in this. I'm not sure what, yet.' He thanks you genuinely.
Prewitt accepts warmly. 'Generosity is its own kind of grace.' He means it.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Prewitt takes it without flinching. 'I've held worse,' he says, and there's no judgment in it — but you sense it's not something he'll keep.
Prewitt accepts it quietly. 'Thank you for thinking of me,' he says. He doesn't expand on his feelings about it.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: Hettie claps her hands. 'Oh, look at that. Look at that! Sit down, I want to tell you something about it.'
Hettie examines it with delight. 'You know, I haven't seen one of these in years. Where did you find it?'
Hettie beams. 'You're very thoughtful, you know. Don't let anyone tell you otherwise.'
Hettie takes it without hesitation. 'A little dirt never hurt anything. Tell me where you found it.'
Hettie accepts with a pleased smile. 'I'll have a bit later, with my afternoon tea. You're very kind.'
Artie nods. 'Functional. Good condition.' He accepts it with the brisk appreciation of a man who values utility.
Artie's eyes widen. 'May I?' He examines it closely, turns it over. 'Where did you get this? Do you know what this is?'
'Natural specimen,' Artie notes. 'Not bad. There's actually some interest in these right now.'

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Artie's mouth tightens slightly. 'I appreciate the thought, but I can't sell something in this condition.' He doesn't take it.
Artie looks at it for a long moment. 'I think we'll leave that where it is,' he says with professional regret.

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: Dot lights up. 'Oh, how sweet! That's the nicest thing that's happened all week.' She means it, and her week is usually pretty good.
Dot accepts it with both hands. 'I'll have it on my break. Thank you — you're very thoughtful.'
Dot beams. 'Useful! I love useful things. Thank you.'

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Dot's smile holds but shifts slightly. 'Oh, that's... you know, I'm not sure I have anywhere to put that right now. Maybe another time?'

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_positive: Sadie goes very still. 'Where did you find this.' It's not quite a question. She holds it at arm's length, then close. 'May I keep it?'
Sadie examines it with genuine pleasure. 'There's something in this. I might paint it. Thank you.'
Sadie tilts her head. 'Well. That's interesting.' And she means it — there's something in the ugliness she finds compelling.
'Practical,' Sadie says, in a tone that's not quite damning but is not enthusiasm either. She accepts.
Sadie takes it without hesitation. 'The texture's interesting. I'm not sure what I'd do with it, but I'd like to think about it.'
Petra grins. 'Oh, I love this. Where's it from? Never mind, I don't need to know.'
Petra takes it cheerfully. 'That's sweet of you. Very you, actually — you strike me as a thoughtful person.'

citizen_item_preferences.reaction_negative: Petra wrinkles her nose gently. 'I appreciate it, I do. But maybe keep that one for yourself?'
